content:
## MARKET OVERVIEW

**1. What is the Current Market Regime?**
NEUTRAL regime — normal volatility (20-day XU100 vol 26.2%) with very narrow participation; breadth 0/8 sectors positive, signalling selective selling rather than systemic panic.

**2. Which sectors are leading?**
Energy & Chemicals showing the least damage week-to-date and contains the best performers: ASELS (Heavy) is the lone leader but Energy names TUPRS (1W: +6.11% | 1M: -1.28%) RECOVERY and PETKM (1W: +5.78% | 1M: +1.58%) ABOVE TREND are the relative leaders within a weak market.

**3. Which sectors are lagging?**
Banking & Finance is the weakest (1W: -12.57%) with AKBNK (1W: -13.79% | 1M: -10.80%) BELOW TREND and GARAN (1W: -13.48% | 1M: -16.00%) BELOW TREND leading the decline; Technology & Telecom and Construction/REIT also underperform.

## STOCK PERFORMANCE

**4. Top Performing Stocks?**
ASELS (1W: +8.73% | 1M: +12.22%) ABOVE TREND; TUPRS (1W: +6.11% | 1M: -1.28%) RECOVERY; PETKM (1W: +5.78% | 1M: +1.58%) ABOVE TREND; GUBRF (1W: -2.69% | 1M: -1.63%) BELOW TREND; SASA (1W: -3.38% | 1M: -10.55%) BELOW TREND.

**5. Worst Performing Stocks?**
YKBNK (1W: -14.77% | 1M: -10.11%) BELOW TREND; HALKB (1W: -14.09% | 1M: -5.52%) BELOW TREND; AKBNK (1W: -13.79% | 1M: -10.80%) BELOW TREND; VAKBN (1W: -13.59% | 1M: -4.12%) BELOW TREND; GARAN (1W: -13.48% | 1M: -16.00%) BELOW TREND.

**6. Momentum Stocks?**
High absolute movers: YKBNK (1W: -14.77% | 1M: -10.11%) BELOW TREND; AKBNK (1W: -13.79% | 1M: -10.80%) BELOW TREND; GARAN (1W: -13.48% | 1M: -16.00%) BELOW TREND; ASELS (1W: +8.73% | 1M: +12.22%) ABOVE TREND; TUPRS (1W: +6.11% | 1M: -1.28%) RECOVERY.

## SECTOR OUTLOOK

**7. Banking Sector Outlook?**
Banking under heavy pressure, weakest sector this week; ISCTR (1W: -12.68% | 1M: -15.23%) BELOW TREND, AKBNK (1W: -13.79% | 1M: -10.80%) BELOW TREND, GARAN (1W: -13.48% | 1M: -16.00%) BELOW TREND, YKBNK (1W: -14.77% | 1M: -10.11%) BELOW TREND — avoid adding exposure absent clear macro or liquidity relief.

**8. Industrial Sector Outlook?**
Heavy Industry soft but mixed: ASELS (1W: +8.73% | 1M: +12.22%) ABOVE TREND stands out while EREGL (1W: -8.03% | 1M: +0.35%) CORRECTION and KRDMD (1W: -3.84% | 1M: -0.73%) BELOW TREND suggest rotation within the group.

**9. Energy & Chemicals Outlook?**
Energy weakest overall but least bad; TUPRS (1W: +6.11% | 1M: -1.28%) RECOVERY and PETKM (1W: +5.78% | 1M: +1.58%) ABOVE TREND show tactical longs, while TRALT (1W: -13.00% | 1M: +5.73%) CORRECTION and HEKTS (1W: -5.21% | 1M: -11.82%) BELOW TREND warn of dispersion across the sector.

## PORTFOLIO STRATEGY

**10. Portfolio Recommendation?**
Neutral tactical stance: trim exposure to large bank positions and weak cyclicals, keep selective exposure to momentum names ASELS and PETKM and to TUPRS as a recovery play; preserve cash to redeploy on confirmed breadth improvement or after capitulation in beaten-down banks.

## NEXT WEEK OUTLOOK

**11. Stocks to Watch Next Week?**
ASELS (1W: +8.73% | 1M: +12.22%) ABOVE TREND for momentum continuation; TUPRS (1W: +6.11% | 1M: -1.28%) RECOVERY for energy-linked flow; AKBNK (1W: -13.79% | 1M: -10.80%) BELOW TREND for potential bounce or further downside; GARAN (1W: -13.48% | 1M: -16.00%) BELOW TREND as a systemic risk barometer.

**12. Sectors to Watch Next Week?**
Banking & Finance for volatility and liquidity-driven moves; Energy & Chemicals for selective strength and relative safety; Heavy Industry for momentum divergence driven by ASELS.

**13. Key Levels & Triggers?**
Watch XU100 reaction to short-term support and any breach of the recent swing low — continued weakness below that level would intensify bank-led selling; catalysts to monitor: CBRT communication, FX moves, global risk sentiment/Oil, corporate updates from major banks and ASELS.

## EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

Neutral market: normal volatility (26.2%) but extremely narrow breadth (0/8 sectors positive) — actionable environment favors selectivity. Opportunities in ASELS, PETKM and TUPRS; main risk remains a bank-led deterioration that could broaden into a correction if macro or liquidity signals worsen.
